message:
refactor(connectors): extract sqlite_db_candidates_from + default_homes_from for deterministic testing

Refactor the two candidate-path helpers added in the earlier
"feat(connectors): cover multiple opencode + pi_agent
installation layouts (issue #174)" commit so the environment-
sensitive parts can be unit-tested deterministically.  Tests
for the old helpers had to manipulate $HOME / $OPENCODE_SQLITE_DB
globally, which made them order-dependent and hostile to
parallel test runs.

src/connectors/opencode.rs

- Drop the thin sqlite_db_path() wrapper that just walked
  sqlite_db_candidates() looking for an existing path — the
  scan() path is the only caller and inlines the walk now.
- Split sqlite_db_candidates() into two layers:
    sqlite_db_candidates_from(home: Option<&Path>,
                              data_local_dir: Option<&Path>,
                              config_dir: Option<&Path>,
                              env_override: Option<&str>)
        -> Vec<PathBuf>
      — the pure, testable core that takes every environment
        dependency as an explicit argument.
    sqlite_db_candidates() -> Vec<PathBuf>
      — the existing public helper, now a thin wrapper that
        resolves the four dependencies from
        dotenvy::var("OPENCODE_SQLITE_DB"),
        dirs::home_dir(),
        dirs::data_local_dir(), and
        dirs::config_dir() before delegating.
- New unit tests exercise _from directly against hand-built
  fake homes + data dirs to verify:
    - $OPENCODE_SQLITE_DB override is the first candidate when
      present.
    - Bare home resolves $HOME/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db
      as the canonical second candidate.
    - data_local_dir and config_dir contribute platform-native
      fallbacks in the correct order.
    - Paths that appear multiple times (e.g. macOS where
      config_dir and data_local_dir collapse to the same
      location) are deduped preserving order.

src/connectors/pi_agent.rs

- Split default_homes() the same way:
    default_homes_from(home: Option<&Path>,
                       env_override: Option<&str>)
        -> Vec<PathBuf>
      — takes the home directory and the $PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR
        env var as explicit arguments.
    default_homes() -> Vec<PathBuf>
      — thin wrapper that resolves both via
        dirs::home_dir() + dotenvy::var("PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR").
- The existing looks_like_root and is_pi_agent_dir matchers
  continue to work as before; only the default-homes discovery
  changed shape.
- New unit tests exercise default_homes_from directly to
  pin the two canonical paths (~/.pi/agent, ~/.omp/agent)
  and the env-override short-circuit.

No behavior change at runtime — both connectors resolve the
same set of paths as before.  The refactor is purely to make
the candidate enumeration testable without touching global
environment state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
